home.kicker: Civic sense isn't about them. It's about all of us.
home.hero: The problem is always other people.
home.heroSub: Not to shame anyone - we've all done at least three things on this page. It just quietly shows who the small stuff reaches: the horn, the wrapper, the spit, the shove. One tap for the real science. Then you decide.
home.everyone: For everyone. No account, no login, nothing about you is collected.
home.count: {places} places · {things} everyday things, every one cited

badge.suggestive: Suggestive - measured in the real world
badge.mechanism: How it works - the size isn't measured here
badge.abroad: measured abroad - shown for the mechanism, not the Indian number

search.title: Search
search.placeholder: A place, a habit, or something you saw today
search.empty: Type a place, a habit, or something you saw today.
search.noResult: No card for that yet - but good one. India's civic life has range.

footer.tagline: A free, non-partisan, open civic-sense project for India. No logins. Nothing collected about you.
footer.disclaimer: Information, never a verdict. Every fact carries its source and the date we checked it.
footer.translation: Translations into India's languages are machine-assisted and still being refined by people. The cited facts, numbers and sources stay in their original words. Found a mistake in your language? Write to us.

kids.cta: Are you a kid? Start here
kids.ctaSub: How to teach your grown-ups the things you learn
kids.title: Hey. You are kind of a superpower.
kids.intro: Grown-ups have done some of these things for years without thinking about them. You get to see them fresh - and that is exactly why kids are the best teachers in the house.
kids.teachIntro: This is the fun part. But nobody likes being told off - so teach the way you would want to be taught.
kids.teach1: Do not say “you are doing it wrong.” Say “look what I learnt today!” and show them the picture.
kids.teach2: Buckle your belt first. Carry your wrapper to the bin. People copy what they see far more than what they are told.
kids.teach3Title: One small thing at a time
kids.teach3: Not everything at once. Pick one. When it sticks, pick the next.
kids.teach4: You have done these things too - everyone has. “I used to do this, now I try not to” is kinder than “you should not.”
kids.teach5: Old habits change slowly. When your grown-up gets it right, notice it out loud. That is how it grows.
kids.respect: Always with love and respect. Your elders have taught you a thousand things. This is just one you get to teach back.
kids.closer: You couldn't have known. Now you do. Now go be the reason your street gets a little kinder.

discover.title: One country, a thousand ways of being it
discover.intro: So many languages, so many festivals, so many kitchens and songs - and somehow, one country holding them all. This is the shared home your civic sense is for. Here is a little of what we are all looking after.
discover.everyone: For everyone. No account, nothing collected about you.
discover.quoteLead: The Constitution asks this of every citizen:

unity.title: Held together by difference
unity.body: India is not one people made the same. It is many peoples who share a country - and the difference is not a flaw to fix. It is the whole idea.

languages.title: A land that speaks in hundreds of tongues
languages.body: Cross a district line and the script on the shop signs changes. None of them is the one true language of India. Every single one of them is.
languages.fact: {scheduled} languages are written into the Constitution. The last census counted {languages} in all.

heritage.title: Wonders the whole world agreed to protect
heritage.body: A marble tomb, rock-cut caves, a mountain railway, tiger-filled mangroves - places so remarkable that the world pledged to help keep them standing.
heritage.fact: {count} places in India are UNESCO World Heritage Sites.

traditions.title: Heritage you cannot keep in a museum
traditions.body: Yoga on a mat, a festival that fills a whole city, a craft passed from hand to hand - traditions that stay alive by being done, not by being stored.
traditions.fact: {count} living traditions from India are on UNESCO's heritage list.

dance.title: Many ways a body tells a story
dance.body: From Bharatanatyam to Sattriya, each classical dance grew in a different corner of the country, and each still speaks its region without a single word.
dance.fact: {count} classical dance traditions are recognised by the Sangeet Natak Akademi.

wild.title: From snow leopards to mangrove tigers
wild.body: Himalaya to desert to coral coast to rainforest - a single country holding a startling share of the planet's wild life.
wild.fact: More than {pct} of the world's wild tigers live in India.

festival.title: A calendar that never really stops
festival.body: A temple bell, a call to prayer, a church choir, the free kitchen at a gurudwara - often on the same street, often in the same week. Someone nearby is always celebrating something.

food.title: A new kitchen every few hundred kilometres
food.body: The same grain becomes a hundred different dinners. Every region feeds you its own way - and every region is quietly certain that its way is the best.

discover.closerKicker: And here is the part that is yours
discover.closerTitle: This is what the small things protect
discover.closerBody: Not with grand gestures, but with the wrapper you carry to the bin, the horn you do not lean on, the room you make for a stranger. A country this varied only holds together if each of us keeps our own small corner of it kind.

home.title: Before the small things, the big picture
home.body: The heritage, the hundreds of languages, the wild places, the everyday wonder - a look at the India your civic sense is for.
